name: gemma4-qa
description: QA over a document corpus. The model is not handed the documents — it must use read-only filesystem tools to find and read the right passage, then answer grounded in the text. Single source of truth for both afchat_lab (Python) and the Aristo app (JS): both load this package and supply only the tool implementations for their runtime.

runtime._comment_max_tool_result_chars: 8000, not 16000: at 16000 chars (~6.4k tokens) per tool result, ~5 of the 8 steps overflow the 32k window on large documents and Ollama silently truncates the transcript — dropping the system prompt and question. 8000 keeps a full 8-step run inside the window; the visible [TRUNCATED...] marker tells the model how to recover.
runtime._comment_num_predict: Bounds a single generation so a temp-0 repetition loop cannot run until the request timeout; ~10x the longest legitimate answer.
runtime._comment_recovery: Loop error-recovery POLICY — the single source of truth for how the agent reacts to a glitchy turn, read identically by afchat_lab (Python) and Aristo (JS) so their two loop implementations cannot drift. Each nudge fires at most once per question. Historically these lived only in the lab's code and were never ported to the app, so the app silently ended on empty/dropped turns; centralizing them here fixes that and prevents recurrence.

recovery.empty_turn_nudge: Your last reply was empty. Based only on the documents you have read, give your final answer now.
recovery.max_steps_final: Based only on the documents you have read, give your final answer now.
recovery.refusal_pointer_nudge: Your last search result was CUT and said more matching lines exist (see its final bracketed note, which names the file). Search that file with path=<that file> and a refined pattern, then answer from what you find.
pointer_markers: refine the pattern or narrow with 'path'

tools.name: list_directory
tools.description: List the documents as a CATALOG: one line per file with the document's title and its main section headings. Call it first and pick the document whose TOPIC (name, title, or headings) matches the question. Use "." or the corpus root for the top-level documents folder.

path.description: Directory to list. Use "." for the root documents folder.

tools.name: read_text_file
tools.description: Read a document's text. Use the EXACT path shown by list_directory. A file can be long; pass head=N or tail=N to read only the first/last N lines instead of the whole file. Output is capped; if cut it ends with a visible "[TRUNCATED: showed X of Y chars ...]" marker meaning the rest was NOT shown.

path.description: Path to the file, relative to the documents folder, e.g. "manual.md".

head.description: If set, read only the first N lines.

tail.description: If set, read only the last N lines.

tools.name: search_content
tools.description: Search for text INSIDE the documents (a content/grep search). Returns the matching lines with their file and line number. This is the fastest way to locate a fact without reading whole files. Matching is case-insensitive substring, so each term is a short exact word/number/unit — not a whole sentence or a glob like '*length*'. To try several wordings at once, pass a LIST of terms in `pattern` (e.g. ["אורך", "length"]); a line matches if it contains ANY of them. Use pattern='## ' to list every section heading (a table of contents), and pass context=N to also return the N lines AFTER each match — search a section's heading with context=25 to read that section's body. If you omit context, each match returns its ENTIRE enclosing table or paragraph plus the nearest section heading — so a value anywhere in a matched table is visible, wherever the matched word sits in it. A search across ALL documents is a LOCATOR: it returns at most a few best blocks per file (most-matching files first, with a '+N more' note) — to read a document deeply, narrow with `path`.

pattern.description: A search term, or a LIST of terms to match any of them (e.g. ["אורך", "length"]). Each term is a short exact substring (a word, number, or unit). Use '## ' to list section headings.

path.description: Optional file (or list of files) to restrict the search to — EXACT filename(s) from list_directory; globs like '*' are an error. Defaults to all documents.

context.description: Number of lines to also return AFTER each match (like grep -A). Use ~20-30 to read a section body after matching its heading. OMIT it to get each match's whole enclosing table or paragraph with its nearest section heading (usually what you want); pass 0 for a compact matches-only list (e.g. with pattern='## ').
